summary.intent: Implement two framework-agnostic input components — RadioGroup (#239) and CheckboxGroup (#248) — as Rust state machine cores in the ars-components crate, following the existing checkbox/switch/toggle_group patterns with full TDD coverage, snapshot tests, spec updates, and mutation verification.
summary.outcome: Both core modules were fully implemented, all quality gates passed (40 RadioGroup tests, 26 CheckboxGroup tests, 0 mutation survivors after triage, clean clippy/fmt/spec/snapshot), spec drift corrected across component and adapter specs, and a ready-for-review PR opened on GitHub with Closes #239 and Closes #248.

repo: The spec_conformance test enforces strict Part enum ordering that must match the anatomy order in the spec markdown; mismatches cause test failures at runtime, not compile time.
cargo xtask spec validate checks 340 spec files and must pass before PR; adapter specs (spec/leptos-components/, spec/dioxus-components/) are in scope and need updating when core API changes.
cargo xci-fast is the required pre-push gate; it may reformat files outside the current PR scope (e.g. unrelated test files), which must be reverted before committing to keep the diff scoped.
cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject requires --features i18n to avoid false orphan failures from the utility/highlight component whose snapshots only exist under that feature flag.

The workspace uses a Bindable<T> type for controlled/uncontrolled value state; SetValue events for controlled prop synchronization must bypass disabled/readonly guards, unlike user-driven Toggle/Check/Uncheck events.

Builder clearer methods (methods that reset optional Props fields to None) are a systematic mutation testing blind spot; they need explicit round-trip tests that assert the cleared state differs from the set state.

The .agents/skills/ directory contains local repo-specific skills (post-implementation-audit, leptos, dioxus, waiting-for-codex-review) that define required workflow steps and must be loaded before their phases execute.
GitHub Project item IDs and field IDs for the board status transitions are stored in MEMORY.md; the Status field option ID for 'In Progress' is 47fc9ee4 on project PVT_kwHOAKgEE84BTlkR.

code.finding: SetValue must not be guarded by disabled/readonly checks because it is the controlled-mode prop synchronization event, not a user-initiated value change. All other value-mutating events (Toggle, Check, Uncheck, CheckAll, UncheckAll) should be guarded.
Roving focus tabindex assignment priority: focused item > selected item > first enabled item. No DOM focus effect should be emitted; adapters resolve Api::focused_item() to live framework refs.

Anatomy conformance tests require Part enum variants in the exact order listed in the spec markdown. RadioGroup needed Description/ErrorMessage moved after the per-item part variants to match spec anatomy order.

Spec previously described platform.focus_element_by_id() for programmatic focus, which contradicts the issue contract requiring DOM-free key-based focus. Spec must describe adapter-resolvable focus by item Key with no core DOM effect.

workflow: cargo-mutants process completion is unreliable to detect via PTY/stdin; artifact files (mutants.out/missed.txt, caught.txt, unviable.txt) are the reliable terminal signal and should be polled with wc -l rather than waiting on stdout.
Running mutation in pre-patch source while patching tests in parallel is valid because cargo-mutants uses its own temp copies; this saves one full rerun cycle when survivors are identified mid-run.

Post-implementation audit should cover adapter specs (leptos/dioxus) in Phase 2, not just core component specs; old granular SetDisabled/SetReadonly event names frequently survive in adapter specs after a core refactor to SetProps.

TDD red-green: add spec_conformance anatomy references first (compilation failure is the red state), then implement modules. This validates that the conformance test itself is wired before production code exists.

Mutation survivor clusters by category: builder clearers need round-trip tests; no-op guards need boundary tests with a case that does and does not short-circuit; per-field prop-change detection needs individual field assertions rather than aggregate ones.

friction: RadioGroup mutation testing required 3 full rerun cycles (~60+ minutes total for 185 mutants) because survivors were discovered incrementally across runs rather than being patchable from a single complete survivor list.
cargo-mutants PTY wrapper kept stdin open after the process exited, making it appear still running; had to cross-reference ps output and artifact file line counts to confirm terminal state.
cargo xci-fast reformatted crates/ars-dioxus/tests/attr_passthrough.rs (an unrelated file) as a side effect, requiring manual inspection and revert to keep the commit diff scoped to #239/#248.
The spec_conformance Part enum ordering failure only manifests at runtime, not compile time, producing a confusing test failure that required reading the conformance test harness source to diagnose.
CheckboxGroup SetValue disabled/readonly behavioral edge was discovered late during audit rather than at spec-reading time; required a targeted patch and rerun of focused tests mid-implementation.
cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject gave false failures without --features i18n due to pre-existing highlight snapshots, requiring a second invocation with the correct feature flag.
open_items: Leptos adapter component for RadioGroup (spec/leptos-components/input/radio-group.md exists and was updated but no implementation; explicitly out of scope per #239).
Dioxus adapter component for RadioGroup (same status as Leptos adapter).
Leptos and Dioxus adapter components for CheckboxGroup (spec updated, implementation deferred; out of scope per #248).
E2E fixtures and widget implementations for both components are deferred per both issue contracts.
Pre-existing rustdoc broken-link warnings in workspace (outside radio_group/checkbox_group modules) were noted but not addressed as they predate this PR.
PR needs snapshot-reviewed label applied after reviewer inspects the 21 new .snap files.
